title: Obstruction Bridge PASS: class 3 (forbidden, v_3=1) — arithmetic obstruction correctly blocks reachability claim
_fixture_purpose: First bridge certificate. The presenting party correctly declares target_arithmetic_class=3 as unreachable (claimed_reachable=false). The bridge validator confirms: v_3(3)=1, so by [111] this class is forbidden — it cannot be the norm of any element in Z[phi]/9Z[phi]. Therefore no PASS cert from [106] QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1 (or its domain instances [105] cymatics, [110] seismology) may assert a control target mapped to arithmetic class 3. The claimed_reachable=false declaration is consistent with the obstruction. Cert result: PASS. This is the canonical bridge PASS: arithmetic obstruction present, reachability claim correctly suppressed.

bridge_claim.statement: Arithmetic class 3 is forbidden (v_3(3)=1). By [111], no element of Z[phi]/9Z[phi] has norm ≡ 3 mod 9. Therefore no valid plan/control cert from [106] may assert a QA target state with this arithmetic fingerprint as reachable. The claimed_reachable=false declaration is consistent with this arithmetic obstruction.
bridge_claim.notes: This is the first instance of arithmetic obstruction constraining a control claim. The claim being certified is a negative claim — the presenting party correctly recognizes the impossibility and flags it. The bridge cert validates that recognition.

validation_checks.details: OBSTRUCTION_PRESENT with claimed_reachable=false: consistent — reachability correctly suppressed
